opportunity_overview.title: National Recruitment Advertising Campaign Services (NRAC)
opportunity_overview.notice_type: Solicitation (Request for Proposal - RFP); Combined Synopsis/Solicitation for Commercial Items
opportunity_overview.agency: United States Secret Service (USSS), Department of Homeland Security (DHS)
opportunity_overview.office: Office of Human Resources (HUM), Recruitment Division (RECDIV); Contracting Specialist: John Akin (john.akin@usss.dhs.gov); Contracting Officer: Erik Syfert (erik.syfert@usss.dhs.gov)
opportunity_overview.posted_date: Not specified explicitly; Amendment 1 issued, original synopsis also provided
opportunity_overview.response_deadline: DISCREPANCY NOTED: Original synopsis (Combined Synopsis Solicitation 70US0926R70093666.pdf) states 4:00 PM EDT, April 28, 2026. Amendment 1 (Combined Synopsis Solicitation 70US0926R70093666 Amend 1.pdf) extends the deadline to 4:00 PM EDT, May 8, 2026. THE AMENDMENT 1 DEADLINE OF MAY 8, 2026 IS THE OPERATIVE DEADLINE.

contract_details.contract_type: Firm Fixed-Price (FFP) Indefinite-Delivery/Indefinite-Quantity (IDIQ); single award; task order-based. FAR 52.216-18 (Ordering), 52.216-19 (Order Limitations), and 52.216-22 (Indefinite Quantity) clauses included. FAR 52.217-8 Option to Extend Services (up to 6 months) also included.
contract_details.set_aside: Total Small Business Set-Aside (FAR 52.219-6 Notice of Total Small Business Set-Aside included; FAR 52.219-14 Limitations on Subcontracting also included)
contract_details.estimated_value: IDIQ ceiling: $90,000,000.00 (total contract ceiling); Minimum guarantee: $1,000.00; Maximum single task order: $30,000,000 (per FAR 52.216-19 Order Limitations); Minimum order: $1,000 (per FAR 52.216-19)
contract_details.period_of_performance: Base ordering period: July 1, 2026 – June 30, 2031 (five-year ordering period per PWS Section 1.2.8 and FAR 52.216-18). Orders may be placed July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2030 per FAR 52.216-18. DISCREPANCY: FAR 52.216-18 states orders may be issued 'from July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2030' (4 years), while the synopsis and PWS both state a 'five-year ordering period' ending June 30, 2031. FAR 52.216-22 states no deliveries required after July 31, 2031. FAR 52.217-8 allows up to 6-month extension of services beyond the base period. BD TEAM SHOULD SEEK CLARIFICATION ON THE ORDERING PERIOD END DATE DISCREPANCY (June 30, 2030 vs. June 30, 2031). A 30-day phase-in/phase-out period is required per PWS Section 1.3.15.
contract_details.place_of_performance: Primarily at contractor's facility; with intermittent access to U.S. Secret Service Memorial Headquarters Building, Washington, D.C.; James J. Rowley Training Center, Laurel, MD; and other USSS-controlled facilities/venues as directed by RECDIV. Contractor travel required CONUS and within the National Capital Region (NCR).

requirements_summary.scope_overview: The contractor shall plan, design, produce, execute, evaluate, and measure the effectiveness of a full-service National Recruitment Advertising Campaign (NRAC) for USSS across all media formats to attract qualified applicants for Special Agent, Uniformed Division, and Administrative/Professional/Technical positions nationwide.
key_requirements: Advertising and Marketing: Multi-channel advertising including TV, radio, digital/streaming, print, social media (LinkedIn, YouTube, Twitter, etc.), outdoor/transit, direct mail, and academia outreach; prime-time ad placement; reach audiences of 10,000+ per campaign
Active Job Boards with programmatic advertising: Capability to reach 70,000+ applicants per year via Indeed, LinkedIn, Clearance Jobs, Zip Recruiter, and similar platforms

Sample Task Order deliverables: Maintain active job channels (41,250 or 52% of new LEO applicants in 12 months); streaming video (84 million impressions/year); streaming audio (140 million impressions/year); pre-roll YouTube (25 million impressions/year)
Recruiting Events: Plan and assist execution of nationwide special recruiting events; propose integrated advertising and special events campaigns
Hosting: Coordinate/procure venue space for Entry Level Assessment Centers (ELACs) when government space unavailable; signage and branding required
Recruiter Sales Training: Annual classroom training for 16+ RECDIV personnel; certify personnel as trainers; deliver repeatable, proven sales process training

Electronic Tracking: Provide talent management technology (e.g., Yello or similar) for event management, candidate pipeline tracking, and career fair data collection via mobile devices (iPad or similar)
Multimedia/Digital: Google G4 analytics or similar reporting; guidance on secretservice.gov/careers website alignment; landing pages for promotional/special events
Promotional Materials and Supplies: USSS-branded merchandise (brochures, cups, pens, calendars, bags, flash drives) for recruitment events
Strategic Planning: Written advertising plans, operational procedures, planning documents, meeting minutes, and status reports
Pre- and post-advertising research and tracking: Qualitative and quantitative research studies, surveys, and media tracking reports
Quality Control Plan (QCP): Draft QCP within 15 business days of award; final QCP within 5 business days after government comments
All media produced becomes sole property of USSS (unlimited data rights per PWS Section 1.3.13)
All contractor employees and subcontractors must be U.S. citizens
Compliance with USSS suitability/background investigation requirements (Tier 1/NACI minimum for Low Risk; Tier 4 Public Trust for High Risk positions)
Compliance with DHS/HSAR contractor employee access and CUI safeguarding requirements (HSAR 3052.204-71 and 3052.204-72)
OCI mitigation and disclosure obligations per FAR Subpart 9.5
Electronic payment via Invoice Processing Platform (IPP); vendor registration required at award
requirements_summary.clearance_requirements: No traditional classified security clearance explicitly required for most positions. DISCREPANCY/INCOMPLETE: PWS Section 1.3.7.2 contains unfilled placeholder text: 'The contractor shall possess and maintain a [Insert level of clearance] facility clearance from DCSA' and 'employees shall have been granted a [Insert level of clearance] security clearance' with 'DD 254 is provided as Attachment [Insert the attachment number].' THIS PLACEHOLDER WAS NOT FILLED IN AND THE DD 254 IS NOT INCLUDED IN THE PACKAGE — BD TEAM MUST SEEK CLARIFICATION. For non-classified positions: Low Risk positions require Tier 1/NACI Public Trust; High Risk positions require Tier 4 Public Trust minimum. Suitability assessments required by USSS Office of Security, Personnel Vetting Division (OSC-PSV) before work begins in USSS facilities or on IT systems; continuous suitability reviews no less than once every two years.
requirements_summary.key_personnel: Key personnel identified in PWS Section 1.3.10 and 1.3.10.1: (1) Project Leader – minimum 4-year degree (BS/BA, preferably Project Management), 5+ years experience in recruitment and advertising/marketing; (2) Market Analyst – minimum 4-year degree (BS/BA, preferably marketing/business/statistics/economics/communications), 3+ years experience in recruitment/advertising/marketing with complex analysis and independent project management; (3) Marketing Coordinator – minimum 4-year degree (BS/BA, preferably marketing/communications/business/public relations), 3+ years in marketing coordination. HSAR 3052.215-70 requires CO approval before removing or replacing key personnel; specific key personnel will be identified at task order level.
requirements_summary.deliverables: Key deliverables include: (1) Draft Quality Control Plan (QCP) – within 15 business days of contract award; (2) Final QCP – within 5 business days after government comments; (3) Post Award Conference attendance (no additional cost); (4) Annual advertising and marketing plan per task order; (5) Monthly reports based on research, target audience opinions, media tracking, and qualitative/quantitative surveys (per Sample Task Order SOW); (6) Pre- and post-advertising research and tracking reports per task order specifications; (7) Branded promotional materials and supplies; (8) Recruiter sales training (annual or as needed); (9) Streaming video, audio, and pre-roll advertising placements per task order metrics; (10) Event management and candidate pipeline tracking via technology platform; (11) Website/landing page guidance aligned with USSS branding strategy. All deliverable-specific requirements defined at the task order level.

evaluation_criteria.evaluation_method: Best Value Tradeoff (FAR Part 15); Government intends to award without discussions — initial offer should represent offeror's best terms. Non-price factors combined are more important than price. Technical Capability and Past Performance are of equal importance to each other.
factors: Factor I – Technical Capability (equal weight to Past Performance; combined non-price factors outweigh price): Evaluated using Confidence Ratings (High Confidence / Some Confidence / Low Confidence). Sub-criteria: (a) proficiency in multi-channel recruiting, especially active job boards with programmatic advertising; (b) innovation in sourcing for active and passive candidates; (c) comprehension of LEO industry, culture, and job requirements; (d) capability to reach 70,000+ applicants per year using active job boards; (e) ability to provide talent acquisition insights and anticipate market shifts; (f) flexibility to adapt and scale recruitment strategies for all USSS roles

Factor II – Past Performance (equal weight to Technical Capability; combined non-price factors outweigh price): Evaluated using Performance Risk Ratings (Low / Moderate / High / Unknown/Neutral). Recency: within last 5 years of solicitation issue date. Relevancy: similar scope, size, complexity — larger is more relevant. Areas: quality, schedule, management. Sources: offeror references, CPARS, SAM, articles/publications. No relevant past performance results in Neutral rating (no advantage/disadvantage).

Factor III – Price (less important than combined non-price factors; not scored/no adjectival ratings): Evaluated for reasonableness, realism, balance, and completeness across all labor categories and ordering years. Sample task order pricing also evaluated for reasonableness, realism, and consistency with proposed rates. Unreasonably high or unrealistically low prices may be rejected. Pricing must cover base year, all ordering years, and FAR 52.217-8 6-month extension period.
evaluation_criteria.submission_requirements: Factor I (Technical Capability): Microsoft Word (.docx), 12 single-sided pages max (excluding cover page and table of contents). Factor II (Past Performance): Microsoft Word (.docx), 6 single-sided pages max (excluding cover page and table of contents). Factor III (Price): Microsoft Excel, unlocked with formulas; no page limit; must include labor category unit prices for all years (base + ordering years + 6-month extension), QCP and Post Award Conference pricing, and sample task order pricing. File naming: [Offeror Name] [Factor Number] [Factor Title]. Formatting: Times New Roman 10-point or larger, 1-inch margins, 8.5x11 paper. Headers/footers within 1-inch margin must include offeror name, solicitation number, and page number. Submission via email to john.akin@usss.dhs.gov AND erik.syfert@usss.dhs.gov with solicitation number, requirements, and offeror name in subject line. Also required: completed representations/certifications (SAM), IPP registration information to ippvendors@usss.dhs.gov.
evaluation_criteria.notes: Non-compliant proposals may be immediately removed from consideration. Government reserves right to award without discussions. Attachment 3 (Past Performance Questionnaire) referenced in both synopsis versions but not provided in this package — BD TEAM SHOULD OBTAIN. Price proposals must use rates from the Direct Labor Matrix (Attachment 4). The Direct Labor Matrix (Attachment 4) as provided contains blank labor rates — offerors must populate all five years for three labor categories.

competitive_intelligence.incumbent: Not specified in any provided document
competitive_intelligence.predecessor_contract: Not specified in any provided document
competitive_intelligence.estimated_competition: Total Small Business set-aside limits the competitive pool to small businesses under NAICS 541810 (Advertising Agencies). The specific focus on Law Enforcement Officer (LEO) recruiting, programmatic job advertising, and the scale (70,000+ applicants/year, $90M ceiling) narrows the field to firms with demonstrated federal law enforcement or government agency recruitment advertising experience. Single award IDIQ means only one firm wins the vehicle. The very low minimum guarantee ($1,000) is typical of IDIQs and does not de-risk the pursuit.
notable_terms: INCOMPLETE SECURITY CLEARANCE CLAUSE: PWS Section 1.3.7.2 contains unfilled brackets for facility clearance level and DD 254 attachment number — critical ambiguity that must be resolved before proposal submission.

ORDERING PERIOD DATE DISCREPANCY: FAR 52.216-18 (full text in Attachment 2) states ordering from July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2030 (4 years), while the synopsis and PWS both state a 5-year ordering period ending June 30, 2031.
FAR 52.216-22 states no deliveries required after July 31, 2031, which appears to accommodate the 5-year period plus option-to-extend, but is inconsistent with FAR 52.216-18's June 30, 2030 cutoff.
Very low minimum guarantee ($1,000) means the government has virtually no obligation beyond the minimum — revenue risk is entirely on the contractor unless task orders are issued.
Maximum single task order is $30,000,000 (FAR 52.216-19), which is substantial and may require significant subcontracting capacity.
All media and materials produced become sole property of USSS with unlimited Government rights (PWS 1.3.13) — no IP retention for the contractor.
Contractor employees must be U.S. citizens (PWS 1.3.7 and HSAR 3052.204-71 Alternate II language) — limits subcontracting flexibility.
FAR 52.219-14 Limitations on Subcontracting included — small business offerors must ensure compliance with self-performance requirements for services contracts.
OCI risk is significant given the nature of work (access to USSS recruitment data, applicant PII, and strategic hiring plans) — proactive OCI mitigation plan may strengthen proposal.
USSS Brand usage is legally restricted under 18 U.S.C. 709 — contractor must obtain written permission from USSS Director for any USSS name usage in advertising materials.
FAR 52.232-18 (Availability of Funds) included — funds are NOT currently available; no payment obligation until funds are obligated.
Attachment 3 (Past Performance Questionnaire) referenced but not provided in this package.

The combined synopsis uses FAR Part 15 (negotiated procurement) procedures for a commercial item acquisition — an unusual but permissible combination that signals the government wants more rigorous evaluation than simplified acquisition would allow.
QASP performance standards are detailed in PWS Part 5: 99% customer satisfaction rate; no more than 3 strategy deficiencies annually; no more than 3 responsiveness deficiencies annually.

bd_action_items.pursuit_recommendation: Best positioned firms are small businesses (per NAICS 541810 size standards) with demonstrated experience in government or law enforcement agency recruitment advertising, programmatic job board advertising (Indeed, LinkedIn, ZipRecruiter, Clearance Jobs), multi-channel digital/streaming media campaigns, and talent management technology platforms. Firms with prior DHS, DOJ, DOD, or other federal law enforcement recruitment advertising contracts and documented CPARS records will have a competitive advantage on Past Performance. A firm capable of demonstrating 70,000+ applicant generation annually and fluency in LEO workforce demographics is particularly well-positioned. Teaming with specialized LEO-focused recruitment marketing firms or digital advertising specialists may strengthen weaker incumbents.
key_dates: April 17, 2026, 4:00 PM EDT – Questions submission deadline (consistent across both synopsis versions)
April 21, 2026, 4:00 PM EDT – Government responses to questions published on SAM.gov (per original synopsis ONLY — SUPERSEDED by Amendment 1)
April 28, 2026, 4:00 PM EDT – ORIGINAL proposal submission deadline (SUPERSEDED by Amendment 1)
April 29, 2026, 4:00 PM EDT – Government responses to questions published on SAM.gov (per Amendment 1 — OPERATIVE DATE)
May 8, 2026, 4:00 PM EDT – AMENDED proposal submission deadline (OPERATIVE per Amendment 1)
July 1, 2026 – Anticipated contract start date / beginning of ordering period
June 30, 2030 – Ordering period end date per FAR 52.216-18 (DISCREPANCY — see below)
June 30, 2031 – Ordering period end date per Synopsis and PWS (DISCREPANCY — see below)
July 31, 2031 – Absolute last delivery date per FAR 52.216-22
Within 15 business days of contract award – Draft QCP due
Within 5 business days after government comments on draft QCP – Final QCP due
Within 5 days of contract award – USSS will initiate IPP enrollment invitation
Upon proposal submission – Submit IPP vendor information to ippvendors@usss.dhs.gov
risks_and_flags: INCOMPLETE SECURITY REQUIREMENT: PWS Section 1.3.7.2 facility clearance level is blank (placeholder not filled). If a facility clearance is required, offerors must understand this requirement before proposing. BD team must submit a question before the April 17 deadline.

ORDERING PERIOD DISCREPANCY: FAR 52.216-18 states orders through June 30, 2030 (4-year ordering period); Synopsis and PWS state 5-year period ending June 30, 2031. This affects pricing for all labor categories. Must be clarified.
MISSING ATTACHMENT: Attachment 3 (Past Performance Questionnaire) is referenced but not provided. Obtain from SAM.gov or request from contracting officer.
AWARD WITHOUT DISCUSSIONS: Government intends to award without discussions — initial proposal must be the best offer. No opportunity to correct deficiencies after submission.
LOW MINIMUM GUARANTEE ($1,000): Essentially zero revenue guarantee despite 5-year commitment. Offerors should carefully model task order probability and pipeline to justify bid/no-bid.

LIMITATIONS ON SUBCONTRACTING (FAR 52.219-14): As a total small business set-aside, the prime contractor must comply with self-performance requirements. Subcontracting strategy must be carefully structured.

PRICE EVALUATION SCOPE: Government evaluates both labor category rates AND sample task order pricing — offerors must price the sample task order accurately and consistently with their labor matrix rates, as inconsistencies can be disqualifying.

FAILURE TO PRICE ALL YEARS IS DISQUALIFYING: Per both synopsis versions, failure to provide pricing for all labor categories for the entire ordering period AND FAR 52.217-8 extension period may result in elimination.
DATA RIGHTS: Government retains unlimited rights to all deliverables including source code — contractors cannot reuse developed materials commercially.
CITIZEN-ONLY WORKFORCE: All employees and subcontractors must be U.S. citizens — limits labor pool and subcontracting options.
OCI EXPOSURE: Access to USSS strategic hiring data, applicant PII, and recruitment planning creates significant OCI risk for firms with other federal law enforcement clients.
18 U.S.C. 709 USSS NAME RESTRICTION: Contractor must obtain USSS Director written permission for any use of Secret Service name/marks — compliance mechanism must be described in proposal.
cross_document_discrepancies: ORDERING PERIOD END DATE: FAR 52.216-18 (Attachment 2) states ordering period is 'July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2030' (4 years). Both synopsis documents and PWS Section 1.2.8 state 'five (5) year ordering period' anticipated July 1, 2026 – June 30, 2031. These dates are irreconcilable without amendment clarification.

PROPOSAL SUBMISSION DEADLINE: Original synopsis states April 28, 2026, 4:00 PM EDT. Amendment 1 states May 8, 2026, 4:00 PM EDT. Amendment 1 is the operative document; April 28 deadline is superseded.
Q&A RESPONSE PUBLICATION DATE: Original synopsis states April 21, 2026. Amendment 1 states April 29, 2026. Amendment 1 is operative.

FACILITY CLEARANCE LEVEL: PWS Section 1.3.7.2 contains unfilled placeholder brackets '[Insert level of clearance]' and '[Insert the attachment number]' — no DD 254 was included in the package. It is unclear whether a facility clearance is required, and at what level.

PWS DELIVERABLE SCHEDULE DISCREPANCY (Minor): PWS Section 1.3.1 states draft QCP shall be delivered within '15 business days' of award. The Deliverables Schedule in Part 4 states '15 calendar days.' BD team should seek clarification; 'business days' is more favorable to the contractor.

CONTRACT TYPE TERMINOLOGY: PWS acronym list (Part 2) includes both 'FFP' and 'CPFF' and 'T&M' and 'LH,' suggesting the PWS template was not fully tailored, though the contract type is stated as FFP IDIQ in PWS Section 1.3.6. No cost-reimbursement or T&M contract type applies to this acquisition.

sections.title: Factor I – Technical Capability
requirements_addressed: PWS Section 1.2.1: Advertising and Marketing – multi-channel media strategy, strategic planning, audience targeting (LEO, Special Agents ages 21-37/40, UD Officers, APT positions)
PWS Section 1.2.2: Recruiting – nationwide special recruiting events, integrated advertising campaigns
PWS Section 1.2.3: Hosting – venue coordination for ELACs
PWS Section 1.2.4: Training – recruiter sales training for 16+ RECDIV personnel, annual or as needed
PWS Section 1.2.5: Electronic Tracking – talent management technology (Yello or similar), candidate pipeline lifecycle management, mobile device integration
PWS Section 1.2.6: Multimedia – Google G4 analytics, website alignment, landing pages
PWS Section 1.2.7: Promotional Materials – USSS-branded merchandise
Evaluation Criteria: Active job boards with programmatic advertising (most important sub-criterion)
Evaluation Criteria: Innovation in sourcing techniques for active and passive candidates
Evaluation Criteria: LEO industry, culture, and job requirement comprehension
Evaluation Criteria: Capability to reach 70,000+ applicants per year
Evaluation Criteria: Talent acquisition insights and market shift anticipation
Evaluation Criteria: Flexible, scalable recruitment strategy adaptation
Sample Task Order SOW: Active job channels, streaming video (84M impressions), streaming audio (140M impressions), pre-roll YouTube (25M impressions)
sections.guidance: Lead with a concrete, metrics-driven campaign framework that demonstrates you can generate 70,000+ qualified LEO applicants annually via programmatic advertising and active job boards, and explicitly map every PWS task area to a proven methodology, tool, or past campaign result to earn High Confidence ratings.

sections.title: Factor II – Past Performance
requirements_addressed: 52.212-2 Evaluation Criteria Section B: Recency (within last 5 years), relevancy (similar scope, size, complexity), quality, schedule, management
Evaluation will include reference checks, CPARS, SAM, and supplemental sources
Attachment 3: Past Performance Questionnaire (must be obtained and completed)
sections.guidance: Submit 3-5 highly relevant past contracts emphasizing federal or law enforcement agency recruitment advertising campaigns with documented metrics (applicant volumes, impression counts, hiring outcomes), and proactively address any negative CPARS findings to prevent them from surfacing as surprises during reference checks.

sections.title: Factor III – Price (Direct Labor Matrix and Sample Task Order Pricing)
requirements_addressed: Attachment 4 Direct Labor Matrix: Fully burdened labor rates for Project Leader, Market Analyst, and Marketing Coordinator for Years 1-5 plus FAR 52.217-8 6-month extension
QCP and Post Award Conference pricing: all labor, materials, ODCs, travel
Sample Task Order SOW (Attachment 5) pricing: active job channels, streaming video, streaming audio, pre-roll, marketing plan, monthly reports — all labor, media costs, ODCs
52.212-2 Price evaluation: reasonableness, realism, balance, completeness; consistency between labor matrix and task order pricing
sections.guidance: Price the sample task order competitively and ensure every line item is directly traceable to your labor matrix rates and a credible media cost basis, as the government will scrutinize consistency between the IDIQ rates and the task order build-up to detect unbalanced pricing.

sections.title: Management Approach and Quality Control Plan (QCP)
requirements_addressed: PWS Section 1.3.1: Quality Control Plan – draft within 15 business/calendar days of award (DISCREPANCY — see flags), final within 5 business days after government comments
PWS Section 1.3.2: Quality Assurance Surveillance Plan alignment
PWS Part 5: Performance Requirements Summary – 99% customer satisfaction, no more than 3 strategy deficiencies/year, no more than 3 responsiveness deficiencies/year
PWS Section 1.3.10: Key Personnel – Project Leader (named, with resume), alternate designated in writing
PWS Section 1.3.10.1: Key Personnel qualifications for Project Leader, Market Analyst, Marketing Coordinator
PWS Section 1.3.8: Post Award Conference and periodic progress meetings at no additional cost
PWS Section 1.3.14: OCI mitigation plan
PWS Section 1.3.15: 30-day Phase-In/Phase-Out plan
HSAR 3052.215-70: Key Personnel designation and replacement procedures
sections.guidance: Name all three key personnel with resumes demonstrating the exact minimum qualifications, present a QCP that directly maps to the PWS Part 5 performance standards and thresholds, and describe a concrete phase-in plan that demonstrates zero disruption to ongoing USSS recruitment operations from day one.

win_themes: Proven LEO Applicant Generation at Scale: Demonstrate a track record of generating 70,000+ qualified law enforcement applicants annually through data-driven programmatic advertising, active job board dominance, and precise audience targeting of the 21-37/40-year-old LEO/military/veteran demographic — directly aligned with USSS's most critical hiring metric.

Full-Service USSS Brand Stewardship: Position your firm as a trusted, compliant steward of the USSS brand across all media channels, with a demonstrated ability to execute multi-channel campaigns (streaming video, audio, programmatic, social, events, promotional materials) while adhering to USSS-approved verbiage, 18 U.S.C. 709 brand restrictions, and all DHS security and CUI requirements.

Adaptive, Intelligence-Driven Recruitment Strategy: Differentiate through a data-informed, agile campaign model — using G4 analytics, real-time applicant tracking technology, and talent market intelligence — to dynamically scale and redirect recruitment efforts across USSS job categories (Special Agents, Uniformed Division, APT) at a moment's notice, reducing government risk and maximizing ROI across the $90M IDIQ ceiling.
